description:
When get()/put() is called on a signing-key backend, or sign()/public_key_der()/algorithm() is called on an NV backend, the error returned is SecretError::InvalidUri. This is the same variant returned for genuinely malformed URIs (bad hex, wrong backend name, unknown algorithm).

Impact: callers using the inventory dispatch (Arc<dyn SecretStore>, Arc<dyn SigningBackend>) should never hit these errors — the factory functions reject wrong-mode URIs at construction. But callers using Tpm2Backend::from_uri() directly and then calling the wrong trait method cannot programmatically distinguish 'your URI was malformed' from 'you called sign() on an NV backend'. This forces defensive code: either always pattern-match on error message strings, or never mix modes — neither of which is enforced by the type system.

This is a minor API ergonomics issue. The current behavior is safe (it errors rather than panicking or producing wrong results) but the error taxonomy is imprecise.

Locations: secretx-tpm2/src/lib.rs lines 257-262, 315-319, 365-369, 428-432, 478-484.
title: Mode-wrong errors reuse InvalidUri, indistinguishable from parse errors
